name: Meadow Creek Homesite
tagline: 8 Acres of Montana Grassland with Creek Frontage
description:
A stunning 8-acre parcel along a year-round creek in central Montana. The land is fenced on three sides with mature cottonwoods lining the creek bank. Rolling grassland rises gently from the water, offering a perfect homesite with panoramic views of the Bull Mountains.

This is genuine Montana ranch country — open skies, abundant wildlife, and neighbors who wave from the road. The property includes a deeded easement for creek water rights, making it ideal for a small hobby farm, equestrian property, or private retreat.

photos.caption: Year-round creek along the eastern boundary

photos.alt: Rolling hills with Bull Mountain backdrop
photos.caption: Views of the Bull Mountains from the homesite

name: Piney Ridge Cabin Lot
tagline: 3 Acres of Wooded Hillside in the Ozarks
description:
Three beautiful acres of mature hardwood forest tucked into the Ozark hills near Mountain View, Arkansas — the folk music capital of the world. A natural spring emerges near the center of the property, feeding a small seasonal branch that runs toward the road.

The lot sits on a gentle hillside with a natural building pad already cleared by previous owners. Towering oaks and hickories create a dense canopy in summer and spectacular color in fall. Electric and rural water are already at the lot line — just bring your cabin plans.

details.utilities: Electric and rural water at lot line

photos.alt: Hardwood forest canopy in the Ozarks
photos.caption: Towering oak and hickory canopy

photos.alt: Natural spring bubbling from mossy rocks
photos.caption: Natural spring near the center of the property
Natural building pad with filtered sunlight
Spectacular autumn color in the Ozarks

name: Sage Flat Ranch Parcel
description:
Forty acres of wide-open Wyoming range land south of Rawlins in Carbon County. The terrain is classic high-desert sagebrush steppe at 6,800 feet elevation — vast, quiet, and utterly untouched. Pronghorn antelope are regular visitors, and the property borders BLM land to the south, giving you functionally limitless open space.

This is one of the darkest sky locations in the continental US. On a clear night, the Milky Way stretches horizon to horizon. At under $250 per acre, this is land banking at its most compelling — Carbon County is seeing steady growth from wind energy development and recreational use.

details.terrain: Sagebrush steppe, flat to gently rolling

photos.caption: Some of the darkest skies in the lower 48
